Operation 'searchPatients' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are minimal. data.summary_suggestion: Search for patients using FHIR criteria
data.description_suggestion: Searches for patients in the system using FHIR-compliant search parameters. Accepts search criteria as query parameters and returns a FHIR Bundle containing matching Patient resources. Supports standard FHIR search operations and filtering. Requires bearer token authentication.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are minimal. Added clarity about FHIR compliance, search parameter usage, return type (Bundle), authentication requirement, and operation scope to help AI agents understand when and how to use this search operation.

Original description is empty. Added context about Group purpose in bulk operations, input schema reference, return value, subsequent usage capability, and authentication requirement for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Create a new patient group for bulk operations
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new FHIR Group resource for organizing patients for bulk data operations. Accepts a CreateBulkFhirPatientGroup request body containing group details and patient identifiers. Returns the created Group resource with a server-assigned ID. The group can subsequently be used for bulk export operations. Requires bearer token authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is empty. Added context about Group purpose in bulk operations, input schema reference, return value, subsequent usage capability, and authentication requirement for better AI agent understanding of when to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all FHIR Group resources available in the system. Returns a FHIR Bundle containing all Group resources with their identifiers, descriptions, and member counts. data.summary_suggestion: Delete a patient group by ID
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a FHIR Group resource identified by the provided ID. Removes the group from the system and makes it unavailable for future bulk operations. Returns a success status upon deletion. This operation is irreversible. Requires bearer token authentication.

data.current_description: FHIR Operation to obtain a detailed set of FHIR resources of diverse resource types pertaining to all patients in specified Group.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a bulk FHIR export operation for all patients in the specified Group. Accepts the Group ID and optional export parameters (e.g., resource types, date filters). Returns a status response with export job details and a polling URL to monitor completion. The operation is asynchronous - use the provided status endpoint to retrieve results when ready. Requires bearer token authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description needs enhancement. Original description lacks clarity about asynchronous nature, parameter details, return value structure, and how to retrieve results. Added context about status response, polling mechanism, and authentication to improve AI agent understanding of the operation workflow.

data.current_description: After a bulk data request has been started, the client MAY poll the status URL provided in the Content-Location header.
data.summary_suggestion: Check bulk FHIR export job status
data.description_suggestion: Polls the status of an in-progress bulk FHIR export job. Requires the job status URL from the Content-Location header of the initial export request. Returns the current job status including completion percentage, output file URLs (when complete), and any error messages. Clients should implement exponential backoff when polling to avoid rate limiting.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic and doesn't specify the domain context (bulk FHIR). Description lacks critical details about required inputs (status URL), expected outputs (status details, file URLs, errors), and polling best practices. Enhanced version clarifies the operation's purpose, input requirements, response content, and operational guidance for AI agents.

message: Operation 'deleteJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary lacks domain specificity and action clarity (delete vs. cancel). Description is incomplete - missing input requirements, response details, state constraints (which jobs can be deleted), and side effects (data discarding). Enhanced version specifies the FHIR context, clarifies the cancellation action, explains preconditions, defines the response, and describes the outcome for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: After a bulk data request has been started, a client MAY send a delete request to the URL provided in the Content-Location header to cancel the request.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an in-progress bulk FHIR export job by sending a DELETE request to the job status URL from the Content-Location header. Only jobs in progress can be cancelled; completed or failed jobs cannot be deleted. Returns a 202 Accepted status on successful cancellation. The job will stop processing and any partial results will be discarded.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks domain specificity and action clarity (delete vs. cancel). Description is incomplete - missing input requirements, response details, state constraints (which jobs can be deleted), and side effects (data discarding). Enhanced version specifies the FHIR context, clarifies the cancellation action, explains preconditions, defines the response, and describes the outcome for better AI agent decision-making.
